design_philosophy.description: A high-density, performance-driven CRM for motorcycle sales. Adopts a 'Control Room' mentality using the Swiss Brutalist aesthetic. Colors are strictly functional, layout prioritizes scannability, and typography uses strong contrasts to establish hierarchy without relying on excessive decorative elements.

layout_and_spacing.card_style: bg-white border border-zinc-200 rounded-sm shadow-sm transition-all duration-200 p-5 md:p-6
layout_and_spacing.sidebar: w-64 border-r border-zinc-200 bg-white min-h-screen flex flex-col

components.inputs: rounded-sm border-zinc-300 focus:ring-2 focus:ring-blue-600 focus:border-blue-600 bg-white
components.badges: rounded-sm text-[10px] md:text-xs px-2 py-0.5 font-bold uppercase tracking-wider

login_hero.description: Modern lounge with red motorcycle. Use this for the image panel on the split login screen layout.

empty_state_vehicles.description: Man inspecting motorcycles indoors. Use for empty state pages like 'No leads found' or 'No vehicles in inventory'.

instructions_to_main_agent: CRITICAL: Write all React components in `.jsx` or `.js` files, DO NOT use `.tsx`.
Import fonts properly. If 'Cabinet Grotesk' is not easily available via Google Fonts, use 'Space Grotesk' or 'Outfit' as a fallback, but strictly avoid 'Inter' or generic sans for headings.
Use 'IBM Plex Sans' (or standard sans fallback) for all body text and UI data.

Layout Approach: Adopt a 'Control Room' dense aesthetic. The main background should be #F4F4F5 (Tailwind zinc-100), with pure white (#FFFFFF) cards. Use a 1px border (`border-zinc-200`) for definition. Avoid heavy drop shadows.

For the Sales Funnel / Kanban board: Make it scroll horizontally. Use light grey (`bg-zinc-50`) column backgrounds. Cards should have colored indicator strips on the left edge based on priority (Red for Hot, Amber for Warm, Blue for Cold).

Lead Creation Form: Utilize conditional rendering. The 'Exchange Info' section must only appear when 'Exchange' is selected. Use Shadcn components heavily customized to match the sharp, low-radius border aesthetic (`rounded-sm`).
Visual Hierarchy: Use uppercase tracking for small labels (e.g., 'LEAD SOURCE', 'PRIORITY') and large, bold sizes for key numbers/names.

Testing Attributes: ALWAYS include `data-testid` attributes (kebab-case, role-based) on interactive elements (inputs, buttons, tab triggers, modal toggles). Examples: `data-testid="submit-lead-button"`, `data-testid="lead-source-select"`.
